feature_name: Dual-mode operation: Root Complex (RC) and End Point (EP)
description: The controller supports both RC and EP roles with a dual-mode core. Mode can be set via bootstrap pins PCIESSMODE[1:0] and overridden in software. As EP it supports legacy or native endpoints; as RC it manages downstream devices. Software sequences for each role are defined. Proper mode selection is prerequisite for link bring-up.
Remarks: Choose RC for host applications and EP for device functions; follow the respective initialization sequence.

feature_name: Link width and speed: x1/x2 at 2.5/5.0 GT/s
description: The link supports one bidirectional interface up to two lanes wide (×2). Gen1 (2.5 GT/s) and Gen2 (5.0 GT/s) line rates are available. Speed change to Gen2 is controlled via PL_GEN2[DIR_SPD]. Dynamic-width conversion and automatic lane reversal are supported for robustness. Throughput scales with configured lanes and speed.
Remarks: Enable Gen2 after stable Gen1 link; verify common clock and de-emphasis settings for signal integrity.

description: Address Space 0 is a 16 KB window subdivided into four 4 KB regions: application registers, local configuration, remote configuration, and remote I/O (RC only). These regions are non-burst and must be accessed as 32-bit transactions. This mapping allows both local control and remote device enumeration. Incorrect access may cause timeouts if the link is down.
dependencies: Base region decode, IOBASE for remote IO
Remarks: Mark these ranges as non-cacheable and avoid bursts; gate remote windows until link is trained.

feature_name: Address Space 1 mapping for data transfers
description: Address Space 1 is used for bulk data movement to remote devices. In RC mode the BAR assignments of endpoints define portions of this space. Transactions to Address Space 1 are translated into PCIe memory TLPs. Software must coordinate regions to avoid overlap across devices.
Remarks: Maintain a software map from internal addresses to device BAR ranges for deterministic routing.

description: Outbound address translation provides 32 equally sized regions (1/2/4/8 MB) to map internal addresses to PCIe addresses. Configuration uses OB_SIZE and per-region OB_OFFSET_INDEXn/OB_OFFSETn_HI. Large transfers must not cross region boundaries; behavior is unspecified if they do. 64-bit addressing is supported via OB_OFFSETn_HI.
Remarks: Choose region size based on expected buffer alignment; split large transfers at region edges.

description: Inbound translation remaps accepted incoming addresses to internal memory using four regions aligned with BAR targeting. Regions associate to BARs via IB_BARn and set start/offset with IB_STARTn_{LO,HI} and IB_OFFSETn. BAR0 always maps to application registers and cannot be remapped. Special handling allows BAR1 value to serve as start address when programmed to zero.
evidence: Section 2.7.2; IB_BARn; IB_STARTn_LO/HI; IB_OFFSETn; BAR0 exception; BAR1 start option
Remarks: Ensure BAR-to-region associations are correct; never attempt to remap BAR0 which is reserved for registers.

feature_name: BAR Mask overlay and DBI_CS2 enable
description: BAR Mask registers overlay BARs to predefine size/type before enumeration. Software must set CMD_STATUS[DBI_CS2] to write mask fields, then clear it and verify via readback. BAR masks are write-only and reads return BAR contents. Misuse from the serial side is unsafe.
Remarks: Program masks early in EP boot; validate by writing test patterns and observing masked bits.

description: The subsystem supports big- and little-endian operation with configurable byte swapping for inbound and outbound paths. ENDIAN[1:0] selects swap granularity (1/2/4/8-byte) when system is big endian. Tables define swap behavior for both directions to maintain data coherency.
Remarks: Align swap mode with DMA width and buffer layout to avoid per-packet byte shuffling in software.

description: The module provides a 128-bit VBUSM slave port for outbound requests and a 128-bit master port for inbound completions or EP-initiated accesses. There is no internal DMA engine; an external EDMA or CPU drives bursts. The design assumes the DMA can sustain ~85% of theoretical link throughput. EDMA examples illustrate configuration for 128-byte payloads.
Remarks: Tune payload and burst sizes to 128 bytes; ensure interconnect and DDR can meet bidirectional bandwidth per lane.

description: Default maximum outbound payload is 128 bytes, inbound is 256 bytes, and remote read request size is up to 256 bytes. These impact throughput and TLP segmentation. Device Capabilities and Control registers allow software to negotiate sizes. Mismatched sizes can increase overhead.
Remarks: Set sizes during enumeration consistent with system DMA and buffer alignment to maximize efficiency.

description: Outbound transactions must be ≤128 bytes, and misaligned starts reduce max size to 120 bytes. Only incremental bursts are supported; fixed and wrap bursts can cause bus lockups. Write byte strobes must be a single contiguous string of 1s. Violating these rules leads to undefined behavior.
Remarks: Configure EDMA to generate aligned incremental bursts and contiguous byte enables across each transfer.

description: The core does not interleave read completions when outbound read size does not exceed the maximum transaction size configured (128 bytes). Larger or split reads may interleave. This affects software assumptions on read ordering and buffer handling.
Remarks: Keep read sizes at or under 128 bytes when ordering is critical; otherwise tag and reorder at software layer.

feature_name: Legacy and MSI interrupt framework (14 lines)
description: A total of 14 interrupt sources are exposed to the SoC interrupt controller, covering legacy INTA–INTD, MSI vectors, error, power/reset, and link events. MSI can be generated and received in EP and RC modes using MSI registers. Legacy generation uses EP_IRQ_SET/CLR and is one-at-a-time until deasserted.
Remarks: Prefer MSI for scalability; for legacy, ensure deassert is issued to allow retrigger and write IRQ_EOI after servicing.

feature_name: MSI generation from EP and RC
description: MSI is emitted via a PCIe memory write to a programmed MSI address/data pair. EP can target RC MSI_IRQ, and RC can target EP MSI_IRQ to signal events. Multiple vectors (up to 32) are supported and enabled per MSIn_IRQ_ENABLE_SET. Status must be cleared and EOI written to accept subsequent events.
Remarks: Coordinate MSI vector assignment during enumeration; clear status then write IRQ_EOI to prevent retrigger.

description: The controller implements baseline and advanced PCIe error reporting with detailed status, masks, and severity control. ECRC generation and checking are controllable in PCIE_ACCR. Error headers are logged in HDR_LOG0–3 to aid fault analysis. Root ports can escalate to SERR on selected errors via ROOT_CTRL_CAP and ROOT_ERR_CMD.
dependencies: Enable bits in DEV_STAT_CTRL and AER registers
Remarks: Disable ECRC when link partners lack support; use masks/severity to match system policy for fatal vs non-fatal.

description: PCIe device states D0/D1/D2/D3hot/D3cold are supported along with link states L0, L0s, and L1 (L2 not supported). ASPM can autonomously transition to L0s/L1; software can also drive L1. PMCMD/PMCFG control PME and L2/L3 readiness. LINK_STAT_CTRL and ACK_FREQ tune common-clock and entry latencies.
Remarks: Balance latency and power by enabling L0s/L1 where latency budgets allow; confirm COMMON_CLK_CFG when sharing REFCLK.

feature_name: Power domain and module state transitions
description: Turning PCIe power domain off/on resets the module, requiring link retrain and software reinit. All traffic must be quiesced before power-down to avoid hangs. PSC GOSTAT must be polled per device guidelines.
Remarks: Assert APP_RETRY_EN and drain OB/IB buffers (ACT_STATUS) before gating clocks/power to prevent deadlocks.

feature_name: Reset mechanisms: POR, system, and hot reset
description: Hardware resets include power-on and system (hard/soft) resets, with sticky bits preserved on soft reset. Software can issue hot reset via RSTCMD[INIT_RST], with a required 100 ms wait minimum. Upstream hot reset requests are surfaced as interrupts for orderly shutdown.
evidence: Section 2.13; RSTCMD[INIT_RST]; PMRST_*; Hot Reset Request
Remarks: After hot reset, reinitialize BARs and translations; observe 100 ms–1.5 s timeouts before retrying TLPs.

description: PIPE loopback enables master/slave symbol-level testing; PHY loopback routes TX to RX internally for TLP loopback without a partner. PHY loopback requires RC mode and specific SerDes configurations, including forcing LTSSM to POLL_ACTIVE via PL_FORCE_LINK. Disable loss-of-signal and set loopback bits per lane.
Remarks: Enter PHY loopback before traffic to avoid sequence number mismatch; ensure BAR windows do not intercept test TLPs.

description: The SerDes integrates a PLL multiplied from a reference clock; enable it via PCIE_SERDES_CFGPLL[ENPLL] and wait for lock indicated by PCIE_SERDES_STS[LOCK]. Reference multipliers (MPY) depend on REFCLK to produce 2.5 GHz line clock. Per-lane RX/TX features include polarity inversion, equalization, CDR modes, and loopback controls.
Remarks: Program PLL and verify LOCK before any PCIESS register access; choose CDR and EQ profiles based on link topology and SSC.

description: Systems may share a common REFCLK to reduce jitter, with SSC typically requiring a common clock. If not shared, software must clear COMMON_CLK_CFG and expect longer training. The PHY generates functional clocks from REFCLK once PLL is locked; accessing PCIESS before lock is invalid.
Remarks: Align COMMON_CLK_CFG to platform clocking; with independent clocks, budget for extended training sequences.

description: Concrete step-by-step sequences are provided for both RC and EP bring-up. They include powering domains, setting mode pins, configuring the SerDes and PLL, disabling LTSSM, programming configuration and address translations, and finally enabling LTSSM. Successful training is indicated by DEBUG0[LTSSM_STATE]=0x11 (L0).
Remarks: Automate sequences with status polling and timeouts; log LTSSM states to diagnose training issues.

description: RC can perform Type0/Type1 configuration cycles to enumerate endpoints using CFG_SETUP and a memory-mapped config window. EP exposes its own config space (Type 0) and supports internal writes to read-only fields pre-enumeration for IDs and BAR masks. Remote IO access is provided via a 4 KB window in RC mode.
Remarks: Only perform remote config/IO when link is up; program IDs and BAR masks prior to enumeration completion.

description: Filter masks allow selective acceptance or drop of TLPs (IO, CFG, MSG), masking of completion attribute checks, and error test relaxations. Controls include SYMTIMER_FLTMASK fields such as F1_CFG_DROP, F1_IO_DROP, and completion test masks. Additional masks handle vendor messages and DLLP abort behavior.
Remarks: Use cautiously in validation or special topologies; do not leave relaxed checks enabled in production unless justified.

description: DEBUG0/1 registers expose link training states, PIPE TX data/K indications, lane reversal, and signal idle status. LTSSM state encoding is documented for precise diagnostics. Additional symbol counters and timers help tune SKP intervals and replay/ACK latencies.
evidence: Section 3.9.11/3.9.12; Appendix A (LTSSM States); SYM_NUM; PL_ACKTIMER
Remarks: Capture LTSSM traces during bring-up; correlate with external analyzer logs to isolate training or electrical issues.

description: The core supports PCIe Message TLPs for transmit and receive. Software can inject custom messages by writing payload to PL_OMSG and toggling PL_LINK_CTRL[OMSG_REQ]. Ensure message routing and semantics comply with PCIe spec and system policies.
Remarks: Validate that downstream fabric permits vendor/standard messages to avoid URs or drops.

description: Completion timeout support and ranges are configurable per DEV_CAP2 and DEV_STAT_CTRL2. Timeouts should not expire in less than 10 ms, with predefined windows from milliseconds to tens of seconds. Proper settings prevent false timeouts under heavy or long-latency fabrics.
Remarks: Select a range matching platform latency; log and mask as non-fatal if recovery is possible.

description: MSI and legacy IRQs are endian-agnostic at TLP level, but software must ensure local register access aligns with system endianness. Status/enable/EOI registers are 32-bit and require write-1-to-clear semantics where noted. IRQ EOI requires writing the specific event number to unblock subsequent interrupts.
evidence: MSI0..7 IRQ regs; LEGACY_* IRQ regs; IRQ_EOI (Section 3.1.15–3.1.71)
Remarks: Abstract register access through driver helpers to enforce W1C and EOI ordering regardless of CPU endianness.

feature_name: Byte strobe and write enable constraints
description: Write strobes must form a single contiguous set per transaction, with no holes. Because PCIe counts in DWORDs, FBE/LBE signals delimit valid bytes at the ends. Violations can cause bus errors or corrupted writes, especially with wide internal buses.
Remarks: Ensure EDMA programming produces contiguous byte enables; align buffers to natural widths to simplify strobes.

description: The PHY exposes REFCLK differential inputs and up to two differential RX/TX pairs. Proper board design must route PCIECLKP/N and lane pairs with controlled impedance. No pin multiplexing exists for functional PCIe pins, minimizing interference risk.
Remarks: Follow TI PCB guidelines for lane length matching and REFCLK quality to meet jitter and eye requirements.

description: The module complies with PCI Express Base Specification 2.0 and integrates Synopsys DWC PCIe Dual Core v3.51a with TI SerDes 1.1.03. This informs interoperability expectations and errata tracking. Feature set aligns with Gen2 capabilities.
Remarks: Use PCI-SIG compliance tests for validation; consult Synopsys/TI notes for any IP-specific behaviors.

feature_name: No built-in hot-plug or L2 support
description: Hot-plug is not built-in and L2 link state is unsupported. Vendor messages and function-level reset are also not supported. System design must not rely on these features for device management.
Remarks: Implement platform-level hot-plug handling externally; avoid relying on L2 or FLR mechanisms.

feature_name: PCIe message signaled power management events
description: Power management messages (PME/PM_TURNOFF/ACK) are supported with interrupts surfaced via PMRST_* registers. EP can emit PME via PMCMD[PM_XMT_PME]; RC can send PM_TURNOFF. Software must handle link reset requests on link down conditions.
Remarks: Debounce power events and coordinate device D-states with link L-states for graceful transitions.

description: Timers and symbol counts can be tuned for SKP insertion, replay limits, and ACK/NACK latency. Registers include SYM_NUM, PL_ACKTIMER, and ACK_FREQ. Adjustments allow optimization for different clocking or jitter environments, but must remain within spec windows.
Remarks: Modify only when required by platform measurements; verify with protocol analyzer for compliance.

feature_name: Security posture: role-based error reporting enable
description: Role-based error reporting support allows advisory errors to be masked by default for compatibility. DEV_STAT_CTRL and PCIE_CERR_MASK expose control. Proper configuration prevents spurious error propagation from devices not aware of role-based semantics.
Remarks: Keep ADV_NFERR_MSK set unless system software implements full role-based error handling.
